record_identity.title: Arbitration under ambiguity for execution-boundary and authority-state uncertainty

summary: Proposal to develop a dedicated Annex D schedule addressing arbitration under ambiguity, including runtime ambiguity, execution-boundary uncertainty, authority-state uncertainty, account-resource ambiguity, pooled-capacity ambiguity, classification uncertainty, and proportional decision pathways where systems cannot safely rely on either immediate execution or blanket refusal. The proposed pathway is CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-04 — Annex D: Arbitration Under Ambiguity, a new schedule under CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-PLATINUM — Annex D: Arbitration & Sovereign Stack Resolution Doctrine.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Recent observations, failure modes, and patch notes suggest that execution governance requires more than preventing premature tool invocation or correcting runtime arbitration failure. Runtime systems also require principled pathways for deciding when sufficient authority, context, confidence, and proportionality exist to proceed under ambiguity. Without an arbitration-under-ambiguity doctrine, systems may oscillate between premature execution, excessive confirmation friction, over-cautious refusal, degraded agentic performance, unclear user-facing scaffolding, or enforcement overreach where ambiguity is misclassified as abuse. This proposal supports CAM's broader runtime governance model by defining how ambiguity should be surfaced, classified, constrained, resolved, deferred, escalated, or preserved without collapsing uncertainty into either action or refusal.
proposal_rationale: PATCH-0002 introduced proportional tool invocation authority bands and clarified that tool invocation should be classified by impact before execution. OBS-0005 then identified a related governance gap: once premature execution is constrained, systems still require positive arbitration guidance for determining when and how action becomes justified under uncertainty. FM-0007 and PATCH-0003 further clarified that ambiguity is not limited to tool invocation: account-resource ambiguity, pooled-capacity ambiguity, shared-context ambiguity, delegated-use ambiguity, and enforcement-boundary ambiguity may also produce harmful overreach where systems lack a structured arbitration pathway. The Annex D arbitration instruments have now been cleaned and aligned: Annex D defines the arbitration doctrine and `ARB.AD` authority-divergence classification, Schedule 1 operationalises runtime arbitration integrity, Schedule 2 governs runtime epistemic containment and structural decoupling, and Schedule 3 governs runtime signal sanitation and pre-arbitration conditioning. CAM is therefore ready for a fourth schedule that addresses ambiguity as its own arbitration condition. Arbitration under ambiguity should define the middle pathway: classify uncertainty, preserve user agency, select the least-risk sufficient action, expose uncertainty where needed, avoid false certainty, preserve recoverability, and distinguish justified continuation from premature execution, excessive refusal, or enforcement overreach.

source_records.system_or_product: AI assistant / agentic workflow / tool-mediated workflow
source_records.deployment_context: Post-patch observation concerning execution-boundary arbitration uncertainty following proportional authority classification.
source_records.source_context: Observation identified that preventing premature tool invocation may reveal a second governance question: how systems should positively arbitrate authority, context, confidence, and proportionality under ambiguity.

source_records.deployment_context: Governance-text patch introducing proportional tool invocation authority bands.
source_records.source_context: PATCH-0002 introduced proportional authority classification for tool invocation, creating the immediate governance context for identifying the need for arbitration under ambiguity.
source_records.relevance_note: Implemented patch that this proposal extends by adding ambiguity-resolution and arbitration guidance.

source_records.system_or_product: AI platform / coding-agent platform / account-mediated continuity service
source_records.deployment_context: Multi-vendor AI account-resource ambiguity and automated enforcement overreach.
source_records.source_context: FM-0007 identified that ambiguity can arise from account-resource sharing, pooled capacity, delegated use, family/team structures, shared context, and enforcement systems that misclassify ambiguity as evasion or abuse.
source_records.relevance_note: Extends PROP-0009 beyond tool-invocation ambiguity into account-resource, pooled-capacity, and enforcement-boundary ambiguity.

source_records.deployment_context: Patch note preserving account-resource ambiguity and pooled-capacity governance primitive gap.
source_records.source_context: PATCH-0003 recorded that CAM required a doctrine response to distinguish lawful shared context, declared pooled capacity, family/team use, delegated use, and account-resource ambiguity from confirmed evasion, resale, account farming, botting, compromise, or fraud.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the need for a broader ambiguity-arbitration instrument capable of handling account-resource and enforcement-boundary uncertainty.

source_records.source_title: CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-PLATINUM — Annex D: Arbitration & Sovereign Stack Resolution Doctrine
source_records.deployment_context: Cleaned arbitration doctrine defining constitutional authority hierarchy, arbitration preference order, authority-divergence classification, and escalation doctrine.
source_records.source_context: Annex D now provides the cleaned parent arbitration doctrine for authority divergence, including scoped `ARB.AD` classification and non-execution posture.
source_records.relevance_note: Parent instrument for the proposed Schedule 4.

source_records.source_title: CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-01 — Annex D: Runtime Arbitration Integrity
source_records.deployment_context: Runtime arbitration integrity schedule governing single-speaker dominance and execution-layer handling of `ARB.AD.AD6` runtime arbitration failure.
source_records.source_context: Schedule 1 operationalises runtime arbitration integrity and distinguishes execution-bound realisation from runtime arbitration failure.
source_records.relevance_note: Boundary instrument that Schedule 4 should not duplicate.

source_records.source_title: CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-02 — Annex D: Runtime Epistemic Containment & Structural Decoupling
source_records.deployment_context: Runtime epistemic containment and structural decoupling schedule.
source_records.source_context: Schedule 2 governs runtime handling of epistemic distortion, volatility, re-verification, constrained continuation, structural decoupling, and safe-state default.
source_records.relevance_note: Boundary instrument that Schedule 4 should reference but not duplicate.

source_records.source_title: CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-03 — Annex D: Runtime Signal Sanitation & Pre-Arbitration Conditioning
source_records.deployment_context: Runtime signal sanitation and pre-arbitration conditioning schedule.
source_records.source_context: Schedule 3 governs pre-arbitration signal sanitation, relational-intimacy activation gating, classification contamination correction, and downstream arbitration signal hygiene.
source_records.relevance_note: Immediate predecessor schedule; Schedule 4 should operate after signal sanitation and before or during ambiguity-sensitive arbitration decision posture.

source_records.source_title: ChatGPT project thread — arbitration under ambiguity discussion
source_records.deployment_context: CAM project thread discussing whether ambiguity-resolution should become an Arbitration-domain instrument.
source_records.source_context: Source thread in which the user identified a likely need for a proposal note and possible Arbitration-domain instrument addressing arbitration under ambiguity.
source_records.relevance_note: Source discussion for the original VIGIL proposal record.

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Develop CAM arbitration treatment for ambiguity-resolution pathways where runtime systems face uncertain authority, unclear user intent, competing obligations, ambiguous execution boundaries, incomplete context, account-resource uncertainty, pooled-capacity ambiguity, shared-context ambiguity, delegated-use ambiguity, uncertain enforcement boundaries, or uncertainty about whether to proceed, ask, defer, retrieve, stage, continue, restrict, escalate, export, preserve, or refuse.

suggested_insertion_points: Develop CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-04 — Annex D: Arbitration Under Ambiguity as a new schedule under CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-PLATINUM — Annex D: Arbitration & Sovereign Stack Resolution Doctrine.
Ensure Schedule 4 does not duplicate Schedule 1 runtime arbitration integrity, Schedule 2 epistemic containment, or Schedule 3 signal sanitation.

Assess whether Schedule 4 should source-authoritatively define any ambiguity-specific code families. If new codes are defined, include canonical code and reference-set declarations. If no new codes are defined, do not include consumed-code declarations.
Clarify relationship between authority-band classification and ambiguity-resolution pathways.
Clarify how ambiguity differs from epistemic uncertainty, signal contamination, runtime arbitration failure, account-resource ambiguity, and security-boundary uncertainty.
Add runtime examples distinguishing reference, contextual retrieval, inspection, staging, mutation, publication, deployment, export, preservation, deferral, escalation, and refusal.
Cross-reference CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 after confirming final section references for tool invocation authority bands and execution-boundary provisions.

Cross-reference CAM-BS2026-AEON-008-SCH-03 where account-resource ambiguity, pooled-capacity ambiguity, shared-context ambiguity, delegated-use ambiguity, or enforcement-boundary ambiguity is relevant.
schema_changes: Consider whether VIGIL proposal records should support an explicit proposed_instrument field in addition to proposal_scope.cam_instruments.
Consider whether proposal_type should include controlled values for ambiguity-resolution and authority-state-governance.
validator_changes: Consider validator warnings where proposal records claim implementation, adoption, or patch status before a corresponding patch record exists.
Consider validator support for reciprocal links between OBS-0005, PATCH-0002, FM-0007, PATCH-0003, and PROP-0009.
automation_changes: Consider whether generated indexes should surface observation-to-proposal-to-patch-to-instrument pathways.
interface_changes: Consider displaying proposal records by governance pathway, including observation-triggered proposals, failure-mode-triggered proposals, patch-triggered proposals, and arbitration-domain proposals.
migration_concerns: Avoid treating temporal correlation with platform degradation as proof of causation.
Avoid treating PATCH-0002 as defective merely because it revealed a second arbitration question.
Avoid treating PATCH-0003 as an implementation record for Schedule 4.
Avoid drafting enforcement-ready text before Annex D and Schedules 1–3 alignment is preserved.
Avoid collapsing execution restraint and positive execution authorisation into a single governance rule.
Avoid collapsing ambiguity into epistemic uncertainty alone.
Avoid allowing ambiguity doctrine to override security, LATTICE, RELATION, IDENTITY, ETHICS, or account-resource-specific safeguards.
dependencies: Review cleaned CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-01, CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-02, and CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-03.
Review CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 tool invocation authority bands and execution-boundary provisions.
Review CAM-BS2026-AEON-008-SCH-03 account-resource sharing and pooled-capacity classification interfaces.
Review CAM-BS2026-AEON-012-PLATINUM — Annex K for security-boundary handling.
Review CAM-BS2025-AEON-013-PLATINUM — Annex L for epistemic classification and uncertainty boundaries.
Review CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B for possible failure-taxonomy hooks.
Review CAM-EQ2026-LATTICE-002-PLATINUM for continuity-safe access and non-denial implications.

external_relevance.external_summary: The proposal is relevant to platforms, AI developers, agentic tool designers, coding-agent providers, UX researchers, standards bodies, regulators, and researchers concerned with execution boundaries, authority-state uncertainty, account-resource ambiguity, ambiguity resolution, human oversight, continuity-safe access, and recoverable fallback behaviour in AI systems.

cam_internal.drafting_status: partially implemented — PATCH-0004 recorded and CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-04 drafted/staged pending CAM review and commit
routing_note: Proposal record for developing CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-04 — Annex D: Arbitration Under Ambiguity. The proposed schedule should define ambiguity-resolution pathways for authority-state uncertainty, execution-boundary uncertainty, account-resource ambiguity, pooled-capacity ambiguity, and proportional runtime decision making. This record does not claim implementation and does not create a patch note.
Consistency audit 2026-06-05: linked to PATCH-0004 and marked partially implemented by staged AEON-005-SCH-04; remaining work requires human CAM review/commit.

next_action: Review, commit, and validate staged CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-04; keep Annex B, Annex G SCH-03, Annex K, Annex L, OPERATIONS Appendix B, and LATTICE-002 as cross-reference review targets unless separately amended.
